title: Closed my short at 2,400 points profit. Next is again a SHORT trade.
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/mkg9yl5zq8og1.png?width=866&format=png&auto=webp&s=1879eace85ff4846019cf0514522fedcd4956eb5

Hey guys,

The short is finally closed. Here's the full update.

Shorted at 70,874 and closed at 68,402. That's 2,400 points of profit. Trade screenshot is attached.

But honestly... it could have been so much more.

At one point I was up nearly 5,000 points. I saw it on the screen. I felt it. Every single part of me wanted to just hit close right there and lock it in. That little voice going "just take it, just take it, what are you waiting for."

But I didn't touch it.

Not because I was being greedy and waiting for more. But because my strategy says close at the time window and not when it feels good. Not when the number looks pretty but at the predetermined time. That was the rule I set for myself and my model.

So I just sat there and watched 5,000 points slowly come back down to 2,400 and closed it right on schedule.

Does it hurt a little? If I'm being honest, yeah it does. Watching that profit shrink in real time is not a fun experience. But the whole point of having a system is that you follow it even when it's painful. The moment I start making exceptions it becomes something else entirely.

Anyway. A win is a win. After that 5,700 point loss last time I genuinely needed this one.



Now the model has given the next signal. Another short.

* **Pair :** BTC/USD 
* **Direction :** SHORT / SELL 
* **Entry :** 11 Mar 2026 at 00:00 UTC 
* **Exit :** 14 Mar 2026 at 23:59 UTC  (4 days | 96 hours)
* **SL/TP** : None, directional call only 



Let's see what happens this time. 



Not any kind of financial advice, just building public records for my model. Do your own research.

comments.author: Equal_Description999
comments.body: A win is a win. There are always more opportunities. Always take profits when you feel like it

comments.author: ddchbr
comments.body: Why would your system hold a trade for a set amount of \*time\*? Honestly curious. Time seems like a strange (sole) criteria for exiting a trade, when you don't profit (or lose) capitcal based on how much the time number as moved. Interested!

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
Because that's my system.. Its a MACHINE LEARNING based model that predicts for the next 4 days.....

So instead of trading fixed rules with SL TP, I trade prediction model......

And I do not intervene manually when I see profits or losses..... My system is back tested on 6+ years of data and it has shown very very good results, for a model which is prediction based. So I trust my system and hold trade for 4 days... 

Yes its hard to see very high profits and then the profits decreases.........

But it's my profitable system. That's how I have backtested it.

replies.author: ddchbr
replies.body: Thanks. I am watching how max hold time affects my strategy and have been considering forcing earlier exits but have not tested that yet. How do you manage your stop loss if you don’t mind me asking (eg do you just leave it alone for the whole 4 days, move to breakeven, etc)?

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body: Yeah I leave it for full 4 days but I set the STOP LOSS according to the average price movement of last 20 days.... The SL distance is so big that it does not get touched, so technically I use no SL to TP.

comments.author: pil666
comments.body: So you did the whole logic on tradingview? You get alerts from there according to the indicator rules and then pass that via LLM for prediction?

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
Nope.......

All on python... My script fetches data from MT5 and then it process it and all the ML thing happens on traditional .py file and then it redirects the signal to my broker.......

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Nice trade — 2.4k points is solid, especially sticking to the plan and actually closing it green. A lot of people let that turn back to breakeven (or worse) waiting for the “perfect” exit.

The “it could’ve been 5k” feeling is the dangerous part though. That’s hindsight bias talking. You never actually *had* 5k — you had unrealized PnL in a moving market. Big difference psychologically.

If this is a repeatable short setup for you, what might help is pre‑defining scale‑outs. For example, take partials at a key level and trail the rest. That way you lock something in while still giving it room to run. It removes a lot of the emotional tension when you see those big floating gains.

Curious — are you shorting into resistance / liquidity sweeps, or is this more macro‑driven? Just make sure the “next short” is based on structure and not on wanting to recreate that 5k moment.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
Nope I trade based on my ML model I developed and tested on 6+ years of data.

I dont see the SND zones or any macro news, I dont even care about price action.... I just trade based on the signal of my model, with discipline.

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
Nice trade — 2,400 points is solid execution, especially on a move that volatile. Respect for actually closing green instead of letting it round‑trip.

That “it could’ve been 5k” feeling is the psychological trap though. Once you’re in hindsight mode, every exit looks early. The real question is: did you follow your plan? If 68,4xx was your level or your system signaled exit, then it’s a good trade regardless of the unrealized peak.

Since you’re planning another short, I’d just be careful about anchoring to the previous high-water mark. Market structure can shift fast after a big flush — we often get sharp short squeezes when late sellers pile in.

Are you trading pure price action or using higher timeframe levels (4H/D) for bias? Curious what’s giving you conviction for the next short.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
My ML system.....

My system is backtested on 6 years of data and it proved it profitability, it outperforms BTC by around 1500%...... also my sharpe is >3............

So I just trade my system, and to be honest, in this #notsure market, it still beats BTC spot.
